title: podman creating improperly configured containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->


```
 podman run -d --name jaeger \
  -e COLLECTOR_ZIPKIN_HTTP_PORT=9411 \
  -p 5775:5775/udp \
  -p 6831:6831/udp \
  -p 6832:6832/udp \
  -p 5778:5778 \
  -p 16686:16686 \
  -p 14268:14268 \
  -p 14250:14250 \
  -p 9411:9411 \
  jaegertracing/all-in-one:1.18
Error: stat /home/deepika/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ffd9ff7408b75314c2fea3331aea9b7ee4be6b54e4f7de30771b869aee82f167: no such file or directory
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

reproduced everytime
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.0.4

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2
  swapFree: 5967097856
  swapTotal: 8392798208

comments.body: @ideepika thanks for the issue report.  Couple of quick hits.  I'm assuming that you're running this as a rootless user?  Are you able to pull the image `jaegertracing/all-in-one:1.18`?  Does this by chance run as root?
@TomSweeneyRedHat yes, no issues there, the container is not able to configure properly.
I've used the same command and it works fine here.  Can you please share the output when you run with `--log-level debug`?

```
$  podman run -d --name jaeger \
>   -e COLLECTOR_ZIPKIN_HTTP_PORT=9411 \
>   -p 5775:5775/udp \
>   -p 6831:6831/udp \
>   -p 6832:6832/udp \
>   -p 5778:5778 \
>   -p 16686:16686 \
>   -p 14268:14268 \
>   -p 14250:14250 \
>   -p 9411:9411 \

also, an observation that might help, this works fine with sudo(root privileges)
do you have `/home` on a different partition?  What does `lsblk -af` show?

sorry missed notification, but still the issue persists

@giuseppe @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat this is blocking in some sense.
I can see the cause being I moved the previous username to a new user and deleted old one, but turns out podman is somehow picking up the old one in creating overlay path: 
`Error: stat /home/deepika/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ffd9ff7408b75314c2fea3331aea9b7ee4be6b54e4f7de30771b869aee82f167: no such file or directory`

while should be `/home/new_username/*`

> 
> also, an observation that might help, this works fine with sudo(root privileges)

have symlinked old username: deepika -> /home/ideepika

when with sudo I get:
```
DEBU[0000] failed to create container jaeger(e50e8dd9d65d3e0e155642d8ec06bd80b69746df133d11b1ee30e7bba92f15e9): the container name "jaeger" is already in use by "0cab192af4338714f7a89a51842dbc979db8b38821f06490b507cff397173d31". You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name.: that name is already in use 
Error: error creating container storage: the container name "jaeger" is already in use by "0cab192af4338714f7a89a51842dbc979db8b38821f06490b507cff397173d31". You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name.: that name is already in use
```
although I have tried to remove the container using it's ID as well:

```
sudo docker rm 0cab192af4338714f7a89a51842dbc979db8b38821f06490b507cff397173d31
Error: No such container: 0cab192af4338714f7a89a51842dbc979db8b38821f06490b507cff397173d31
```
You will need to `podman rm --storage` the ID if it does not appear in `podman ps` but Podman complains about it existing

podman ps -a --storage
now exists.  Working on adding the ability to remove specific container images.
